name: Security Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank in Oklahoma offering personal and business banking services with a focus on relationship-based, local banking.
description_long: Security Bank operates as a community-focused financial institution based in Tulsa, Oklahoma, with origins rooted in a small office approach emphasizing personal relationships over transactional banking. The bank explicitly positions itself as a "people-first" institution that prioritizes meaningful partnerships with customers and maintains a commitment to preserving community banking culture. Their service offerings span personal banking products, business banking solutions, business lending, and digital banking capabilities, with emphasis on providing direct support and personalized financial guidance. Security Bank distinguishes itself through its commitment to local community investment and relationship banking rather than a national or digital-first model. The bank maintains FDIC insurance on deposits and operates with traditional branch hours (Tulsa main branch: 9 AM-4 PM lobby; 7:30 AM-6 PM drive-thru weekdays). While positioning as a personal, accessible alternative to larger banks, Security Bank's geographic footprint appears limited to Oklahoma (Tulsa main branch and Edmond loan production office), which restricts accessibility for customers outside this region. Their digital offerings exist but appear secondary to their relationship-banking emphasis, and the website provides limited transparency on specific product rates, terms, loan amounts, or competitive positioning against regional or national alternatives. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: ATM access through Transfund and QuikTrip partnerships
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Extended drive-thru hours (7:30 AM-6 PM weekdays; 9 AM-12 PM Saturday)
24/7 telephone banking line for account access
Surcharge-free ATM access through QuikTrip (65+ locations) and Transfund network (200+ Tulsa metro locations)
Offers both personal and business banking with business lending services
Mobile app available for digital banking
Local relationship-based banking model with direct banker support
Remote deposit and lockbox services available
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to Oklahoma locations only (Tulsa and Edmond)
Website provides no transparent disclosure of interest rates, APYs, loan terms, or fees
Oklahoma City location is loan production office only—cannot accept deposits or process withdrawals
Limited information about specific product offerings, minimum balances, or account types available
No online account opening process visible on website; requires visit or call to open accounts
best_for: Oklahoma-based individuals and small business owners seeking personalized banking relationships
Local business owners needing business lending and streamlined banking operations
Customers who prefer traditional community banking over large national or digital-only banks
Businesses in Tulsa and surrounding areas requiring business banking and lending services
diagnosis: Security Bank is best for Oklahoma-based individuals and small business owners who prioritize relationship-based banking and local community investment over digital convenience or competitive product rates. Primary caveat: the bank's transparency is limited regarding specific product terms, rates, and fees, and its geographic availability is restricted to Oklahoma, making it inaccessible for out-of-state customers.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
